title: China-to-Morocco Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: ONEE Grid-Connection Requirements and Loi 13-09 Renewable-Energy Framework
rows.target_requirement: Morocco's national electricity utility ONEE (Office National de l'Electricité et de l'Eau Potable) is the grid operator and publishes grid-connection technical rules for power plants and distributed generation. Loi 13-09 (as amended by loi 58-15) establishes the legal basis for renewable energy production and grid access. Self-generation (autoproduction) is governed by Loi 82-21 (relative à l'autoproduction d'énergie électrique), which establishes three tiers: declaration required for installations below 11 kW; approval required for 11 kW to 5 MW; authorisation required above 5 MW. Loi 47-09 covers energy efficiency and earlier net-metering arrangements but does not set the current self-generation thresholds. Grid-connected inverters must meet ONEE's technical requirements for voltage and frequency ride-through, anti-islanding, reactive-power control, protection coordination, power quality, and metering. ONEE may require project-specific studies for larger installations.
target_standards: Loi 13-09 relative aux énergies renouvelables (as amended by loi 58-15)
Loi 82-21 relative à l'autoproduction d'énergie électrique (self-generation tiers: <11 kW declaration, 11 kW–5 MW approval, >5 MW authorisation)
Loi 47-09 relative à l'efficacité énergétique (energy efficiency; earlier net-metering context)
ONEE technical grid-connection rules for distributed generation and renewable power plants
Moroccan Electricity Distribution Code
rows.china_equivalent: China grid-connection evidence for PV inverters commonly cites GB/T 19964 (technical requirements for PV power stations connecting to power systems) and GB/T 37408. These standards address similar technical areas but are written for the Chinese 50 Hz grid and regulatory framework; they are not acceptable as ONEE grid-connection studies or Morocco project-approval documentation.
rows.gap: Prepare a Morocco-specific ONEE grid settings sheet covering: voltage and frequency ride-through windows for the 50 Hz Moroccan grid, reactive-power Q(U) and Q(P) behaviour, anti-islanding method, reconnection delay, active power curtailment interface, protection relay settings, metering point, and power quality compliance. Submit project-specific grid studies for installations above the distributed-generation threshold. Determine the applicable registration route under Loi 82-21 (declaration / approval / authorisation) based on installed capacity, and confirm loi 13-09 licence requirements with ONEE and the Moroccan energy regulator (ANRE).

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected PV inverters under ONEE technical rules; loi 13-09 / loi 47-09 license or declaration is mandatory for the project as a whole
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for Chinese grid-connected PV projects under CNCA and NB/T 32004; not transferable as ONEE grid approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 19964 grid documentation does not satisfy ONEE grid-connection approval. Prepare Morocco-specific ONEE settings and project-specific studies, and confirm the applicable registration tier under Loi 82-21 and loi 13-09 licence route before commissioning.

source.publisher: Office National de l'Electricité et de l'Eau Potable (ONEE)

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Frequency and Voltage Settings for Morocco
rows.target_requirement: Morocco operates a 50 Hz grid with low-voltage nominal voltage of 220 V (single phase) and 400 V (three phase), interconnected with the European ENTSO-E synchronous area via submarine cable links to Spain. Inverters must be configured for 50 Hz operation, and frequency ride-through, trip thresholds, reconnection delay, and power-reduction gradients should follow ONEE technical rules and European / IEC practice, not Chinese GB/T settings calibrated for the Chinese 50 Hz grid's distinct frequency and voltage tolerance bands.
target_standards: ONEE technical grid-connection requirements for distributed PV generation
IEC 61727 (PV systems — characteristics of the utility interface)
ENTSO-E Network Code on Requirements for Generators (NC RfG) — relevant for larger plants seeking export or cross-border context
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters default to settings under GB/T 37408 and GB/T 19964 for the Chinese 50 Hz grid. While the nominal frequency matches, trip windows, reconnection sequences, reactive-power curve shapes, and active-power response ramps may differ from ONEE or ENTSO-E expectations. Chinese inverters typically support multi-market configuration; firmware parameter adjustment for the Morocco market is necessary.
rows.gap: Confirm that the inverter model supports a Morocco / European 50 Hz grid profile. Provide ONEE or project engineer with a firmware settings sheet showing voltage and frequency trip thresholds, reconnection delay, reactive-power curve, and any active-power reduction ramp. Do not ship with factory-default China-only settings without verification against ONEE requirements.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory parameter compliance for grid connection under ONEE technical rules; firmware settings are a project-commissioning requirement
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory under Chinese grid-connection inspection and acceptance; China settings are not directly transferable

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] 50 Hz frequency is shared between China and Morocco, but grid parameter windows differ. Provide an ONEE-compatible settings sheet and confirm inverter firmware supports the Moroccan grid profile before commissioning.

source.publisher: Office National de l'Electricité et de l'Eau Potable (ONEE)

rows.requirement: Morocco Renewable-Energy Market Access and ADEREE / AMEE Policy Context
rows.target_requirement: Morocco has adopted an ambitious renewable-energy programme targeting 52 percent renewable capacity by 2030, driven by MASEN (Moroccan Agency for Sustainable Energy) for large utility-scale projects and AMEE (Agence Marocaine pour l'Efficacité Energétique) for energy efficiency and distributed generation. Importers of PV inverters must comply with Moroccan customs requirements including HS code classification, any applicable import duties, and conformity documentation required by the Ministry of Industry or relevant arrêté. IMANOR conformity marks or CE marking (as a proxy indicator) may be required or expected by procurement rules for public projects, IFC or World Bank-financed projects, or private offtakers.
target_standards: Loi 13-09 relative aux énergies renouvelables (as amended)
Décret de loi 47-09 relative à l'efficacité énergétique
Moroccan Customs Tariff and import regulations
MASEN project procurement specifications for utility-scale solar
rows.china_equivalent: China PV inverters exported to Morocco are classified under HS 8504.40 (static converters) for customs purposes. Chinese export documentation (CCC exemption letter, GB/T 37408 test report, factory inspection certificate) is not a substitute for Moroccan IMANOR conformity, MASEN procurement requirements, or customs conformity attestations. Exporters should confirm whether a Moroccan ATA carnet or temporary import route applies for demonstration units.
rows.gap: Confirm HS code classification with a Moroccan customs broker. Establish whether IMANOR conformity mark or a recognised third-party test certificate is required for customs clearance or the relevant public-procurement or project-finance requirement. Engage MASEN or AMEE early if the project falls under national renewable or efficiency programmes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory customs compliance (HS classification, duties, conformity attestation as required); MASEN / public procurement requirements are project-specific and may mandate IEC or IMANOR evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese export requires standard customs export documentation; GB/T 37408 is mandatory for Chinese market but not for export documentation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Morocco is a significant and growing solar market. Chinese exporters should engage a local agent or customs broker to confirm current import conformity requirements, customs duties, and MASEN or AMEE procurement standards before the first shipment.

rows.requirement: CN-MA Free Trade and Preferential Tariff Context for Solar Inverters
rows.target_requirement: Morocco has a free-trade agreement with the EU (Association Agreement) and bilateral FTAs with the USA, Egypt, Jordan, Tunisia, and others. Morocco does not have an FTA with China as of 2026. Standard import duties for HS 8504.40 static converters apply for Chinese-origin goods entering Morocco under the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) rate. Moroccan import duties on solar PV inverters can vary and may be affected by domestic industrialisation policy. Importers should confirm the current MFN rate, value-added tax, and any parafiscal levies with the Moroccan customs authority (ADII — Administration des Douanes et Impôts Indirects).
target_standards: Moroccan Customs Tariff (ADII)
HS 8504.40 MFN import duty rate
Moroccan VAT on imports
Loi de finances (annual finance law — may adjust duty rates)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter exports to Morocco use standard export customs procedures under MOFCOM / GACC rules. China certificate of origin (Form A or self-certification) does not confer preferential tariff treatment in Morocco as no GSP or FTA rate applies for Chinese-origin goods under the Moroccan tariff schedule as of 2026.
china_standards: China MOFCOM / GACC export customs rules
rows.gap: No FTA rate is available for Chinese-origin solar inverters entering Morocco. Confirm the current MFN import duty rate for HS 8504.40 with ADII or a licensed Moroccan customs broker. Include Morocco import duty and VAT in the landed-cost model. Verify whether Moroccan government programmes offer duty exemptions for renewable-energy equipment on a project basis.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory to comply with Moroccan customs import duty and VAT obligations; duty exemptions are project-specific and must be confirmed with ADII
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese export customs compliance is mandatory; no FTA preference applies for Morocco

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] No China–Morocco FTA applies as of 2026. Confirm current MFN import duty for HS 8504.40 and any project-level duty exemptions with ADII or a licensed customs broker before pricing the export.

source.publisher: Administration des Douanes et Impôts Indirects (ADII), Morocco

rows.requirement: IMANOR NM / IEC 62109 Inverter Safety Certification
rows.target_requirement: IMANOR, Morocco's national standardisation body, adopts IEC standards as Moroccan National Standards (NM). Solar PV inverters are expected to meet IEC 62109-1 (safety for power converters — general requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (safety for PV inverters) as adopted into the NM framework as NM EN 62109-1 and NM EN 62109-2. Loi 24-09 (relative à la sécurité des produits et des services) and Arrêté No. 2573-14 mandate that electrical equipment placed on the Moroccan market must bear the CMIM mark (Cم), the Moroccan conformity mark equivalent, before sale or installation. IMANOR can issue conformity marks or accept third-party test reports from accredited laboratories against these standards.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 as adopted by IMANOR (NM EN 62109-1) (NM framework)
IEC 62109-2 as adopted by IMANOR (NM EN 62109-2) (NM framework)
Loi 24-09 relative à la sécurité des produits et des services (product safety law mandating market conformity)
Arrêté No. 2573-14 (low-voltage electrical equipment — CMIM mark requirement)
Moroccan ministerial arrêtés on electrical equipment market surveillance
rows.china_equivalent: China solar PV inverter safety is governed by GB/T 37408 (technical specification for PV inverters) and supplementary NB/T or T/CPIA group standards. GB/T 37408 covers protection and safety functions but is not identical in scope or test methodology to IEC 62109-1/2. A Chinese test report against GB/T 37408 is not directly accepted by IMANOR or a Moroccan project owner without IEC-route evidence.
rows.gap: Obtain or commission test reports from an accredited laboratory against IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2, and prepare a declaration of conformity or IMANOR conformity-mark application for the Morocco market. Chinese GB/T 37408 test data may support a gap analysis but does not replace IEC-route evidence. Confirm with IMANOR or the Moroccan project owner whether an arrêté mandates certification before sale or installation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where a ministerial arrêté designates the product; IEC 62109 compliance is the accepted technical route via IMANOR NM adoption; project-owner or lender requirements may make it de facto mandatory
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for grid-connected PV inverters under Chinese market surveillance; GB/T 37408 is the applicable standard

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T 37408 test evidence is a useful starting point but does not satisfy the IMANOR / IEC 62109 route expected for Morocco. Commission IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 testing from an accredited laboratory before shipment.

rows.requirement: EMC and Electrical Safety for PV Power Converters in Morocco
rows.target_requirement: Morocco applies IEC-based electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) standards through IMANOR NM adoption. Power conversion equipment including PV inverters should meet relevant IEC CISPR conducted and radiated emission limits and immunity requirements. Low-voltage electrical safety for equipment placed on the Moroccan market is governed by Arrêté No. 2573-14 (low-voltage) and Arrêté No. 2574-14 (EMC), which require conformity to the CMIM mark (Cم) for designated categories. Electrical installation rules follow IEC 60364 as adopted by IMANOR. Moroccan projects in public-sector programmes may require CE marking-equivalent documentation as a reference, particularly for EU-funded or IFC / World Bank projects.
target_standards: IEC CISPR 11 / CISPR 32 as adopted via IMANOR NM
Order No. 2573-14 (Arrêté No. 2573-14 — low-voltage electrical safety requirements for electrical equipment)
Order No. 2574-14 (Arrêté No. 2574-14 — EMC requirements for electrical equipment)
IEC 60364 installation rules as adopted by IMANOR
rows.china_equivalent: China PV inverters are tested for EMC under GB/T 17626 and GB 4824 series (CISPR 11 equivalents), and electrical safety under GB 4943 and related standards. These Chinese test results give useful technical evidence but are not equivalent to IMANOR NM or IEC CISPR certifications for the Morocco market.
rows.gap: Supplement Chinese EMC test evidence with IEC CISPR-route tests from an accredited laboratory if the project owner, lender, or IMANOR conformity review requires it. For EU-funded projects, CE marking under the EU EMC Directive and Low Voltage Directive may be specifically requested.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where a Moroccan arrêté designates the product for conformity; IEC CISPR / IEC 60364 compliance is the accepted technical route; project or lender requirements may be more specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory under Chinese CCC or voluntary certification routes; GB 4824 / GB/T 17626 are the applicable EMC standards

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese EMC test reports are technically comparable but not directly accepted as IMANOR NM conformity evidence. Obtain IEC CISPR-route test evidence from an accredited laboratory if the project or procurement requires it.
